$schema_note: Resourcing, timeline, budget, and risk register TEMPLATE. Unlike files 00-11 (which are structurally complete from definition_data), almost everything here is a placeholder — it exists purely so you have a fixed shape to fill in once you've answered SystemDefinition5.txt. Nothing in this file was inferable from definition_data or docs/index.html.

milestones.name: Auth + OSAS + Catalog usable end-to-end (P1.AUTH, P1.OSAS, P1.CATALOG)
QR attendance engine working in staging (P1.QR)
Notifications, Settings, Audit complete; feature-complete for Phase 1
Crosscutting QA/security gates green (coverage targets, OWASP checklist)
Staging UAT signed off by Department Admin

estimation_conventions.note: Every task object across 01-11 has estimate.value=null — populate per-task once the team and methodology above are fixed. Do not attempt to fill these before team/methodology decisions land; an estimate without a known assignee/skill-level is guesswork twice over.

risk_register_template.risk: Real OSAS API contract/credentials arrive late or differ from the assumed shape
risk_register_template.mitigation: Build entirely against the mock OSAS fixture server (P0.DB.INT01) so development is never blocked; integrate the real API as a late-binding swap.

risk_register_template.risk: QR/attendance engine (P1.QR) is under-tested and ships with a fraud or false-absence bug
risk_register_template.mitigation: Enforce the 90%+ coverage gate and k6 concurrency test before this work package can be marked done (P1.QR.QA01, P1.QR.QA02).

risk_register_template.risk: Hosting/domain/budget decisions stall Phase 0 infra tasks
risk_register_template.mitigation: Answer SystemDefinition5.txt §8 and §13 before Phase 0 starts, not during it.

risk_register_template.risk: Solo-developer or very small team cannot sustain the full crosscutting QA/security discipline (07) alongside feature work
risk_register_template.mitigation: Scope down non-negotiables (attendance/auth coverage, OWASP checklist) vs. nice-to-haves (full E2E matrix) explicitly, rather than silently skipping.

risk_register_template.risk: Campus Wi-Fi reliability during class hours undermines the sub-1s scan round-trip NFR
risk_register_template.mitigation: Manual attendance fallback (P1.QR.BE06) is the documented universal fallback — confirm faculty are trained on it before go-live.

how_to_fill: 2. Fill team, methodology, timeline, budget, environments sections above from those answers.
3. Walk files 01-08 in order, filling estimate/priority/assignee/status/dates on each task using the now-known team capacity and target dates.
4. Import the filled tasks into whatever project-management tool §3/§1 of SystemDefinition5.txt names — this WBS remains the versioned source of truth in git; the PM tool is the daily-use view.
5. Revisit the risk_register_template regularly (weekly per maintenance-definition.json cadence, at minimum) — add risks as they're discovered, don't just fill it once.
